defs.def: not, lack of, the opposite of see also non- (def. 1)

back; the reverse or removal of (added to verbs to indicate a reversal of the action and to nouns to indicate a removal or release of the thing mentioned or from the condition, place, etc. indicated, and sometimes used merely as an intensive [unbind, unfold; unbonnet, unbosom; unloose])

def_groups.semantics: The following list includes many of the more common compounds formed with un- (either sense) that do not have special meanings

examples.example: The panic slowly subsided to un- easy apprehension while the minutes crawled by as if they were each an hour long.
